topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments and acts that modify existing laws across different sectors, including finance, agriculture, taxation, food safety, disability, and customs.

Document 1: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual written privacy policy notice requirement any financial institution
provides customers access to such most recent disclosure in electronic or other form permitted by specified regulations.
Document 2: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to carry out a Community-Supported Agriculture Promotion program
Provides Commodity Credit Corporation (CCC) funding for the program through FY2018.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2014 the tax credit for differential wage payments
to employees who are active duty members of the uniformed services.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the Commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration from using federal funds to amend specified regulations
to include dry ultra-filtered milk, milk protein concentrate, or casein in the definition of 'milk' or 'nonfat milk'.
Document 5: Abolishes the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and each Federal reserve bank
Repeals the Federal Reserve Act.
Document 6: The Commissioner of Social Security shall prescribe rules and regulations that update medical-vocational guidelines
used in disability determinations.
Document 7: Directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to provide for the reliquidation (refund of duties) of certain entries
of polyester fleece sheet sets.

topic.name: Medicare and Prescription Drug Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming Medicare and improving access to affordable prescription drugs and related healthcare services.

Document 1: Affordable Medicines Utilization Act of 2011 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
require an increase in the quarterly Medicaid payment to a state by 50% of the generic drug utilization savings amount
Document 2: Medicare Prescription Drug Savings and Choice Act of 2015
require the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to offer one or more Medicare-operated prescription drug plan options
Document 3: Medicare DMEPOS Competitive Acquisition Reform Act of 2008
delay generally until after 2011 full implementation of the Medicare competitive acquisition program
Document 4: Clinical Social Work Medicare Equity Act of 2009
exclude clinical social worker services from coverage under the Medicare skilled nursing facility
Document 5: Seniors' Rights to Know Act
require the information the Secretary of Health and Human Services provides to promote informed choice on available part C coverage options
Document 6: Medicare Fair Drug Pricing Act of 2015
require the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to negotiate with drug manufacturers
Document 7: Medicare Hospice Care Access Act of 2011
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving education access, quality, and outcomes for diverse student populations in the United States.

Document 1: amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965
prohibits ED from waiving statutory provisions that ban Pell Grant eligibility for incarcerated students.
Document 2: authorizes the Director of the National Academic and Career Innovation Center to award grants
create and enhance educational and career pathways for high school students.
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an in-school facility innovation program contest
amends part A of title VII of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to establish a program awarding grants to local educational agencies.
Document 5: directs the Administrator of the EPA to review and publish the Radon Measurements In Schools Guidelines
requires the Administrator to award grants to states to conduct short-term tests of radon levels in schools.
Document 6: authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools
requires such schools to provide access to educational and health services.
Document 7: authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states to improve access to education data
requires state grantees to implement integrated statewide education longitudinal data systems.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Documents discussing amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding temporary duty suspensions and reductions on various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on subassemblies for instruments or apparatus for measuring or checking electrical quantities.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers, not dyed and not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to reduce temporarily the duty on certain metal iodide pellets.
reduce temporarily the duty
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary reduction of duty on rubber basketballs.
temporary reduction of duty
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on sports footwear for women.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on check valves of steel.
suspend temporarily the duty
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a certain chemical.
temporary suspension of duty

topic.name: Veterans Health Care and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care access, mental health support, and burial services for veterans.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure that veterans have access to hospital care and medical services.
Directs the Secretary to report to Congress on compliance with such requirement.
Document 2: Establishes a commission on veterans and members of the Armed Forces with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).
Requires the commission to oversee the monitoring and treatment of veterans with such disorders.
Document 3: Authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs to furnish a casket or urn for the burial of a deceased veteran.
Determines resources for the burial of such veteran in a national cemetery.
Document 4: Reduces the period for training program completion for veterans' outreach program specialists.
Mandatory training for local veterans' employment representatives.
Document 5: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to issue a veterans health care stamp to help fund medical care for veterans.
Document 6: Amends the Older Americans Act to include status as a veteran among categories of greatest social need.
Requires state plans to engage in outreach to veterans eligible for services.
Document 7: Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act to require reimbursement for health care services provided to veterans.
Ensures reimbursement even without the veteran's medical records.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislation related to energy production, regulation, and environmental considerations, particularly in the context of oil, gas, and alternative energy sources.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify and set forth provisions concerning tax credits for alternative and renewable energy
Establishes the American-Made Energy Trust Fund.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Energy from issuing a permit to drill for a covered well unless the applicant demonstrates safety measures
Defines 'covered well' to mean an oil or gas exploration or production well that could lead to extensive harm.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to include hydrogen sulfide in the list of hazardous air pollutants
Repeal the prohibition on aggregating emissions from oil or gas exploration or production wells.
Document 4: Declares without force all federal prohibitions against natural gas leasing in the Outer Continental Shelf
Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for oil and natural gas exploration.
Document 5: Declares as having no force all federal prohibitions against the leasing of any area of the outer Continental Shelf
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to inventory the oil and natural gas resources of all submerged lands.
Document 7: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act to redefine the term 'Alaska natural gas transportation project'
Confers upon the Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects responsibility for providing federal agencies with information.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of immigration law, enforcement, and assistance for specific groups, including children and military personnel.

Document 1: Authorizes states to enforce immigration criminal penalties
Requires states to provide DHS with information about apprehended aliens.
Document 2: Requires state plans to assist alien children in achieving lawful permanent resident status
Authorizes grants to assist children in the child welfare system.
Document 3: Eligibility for naturalization for members of the U.S. Armed Forces
Prohibits removal of certain military personnel based on immigration status.
Document 4: Amends provisions related to the definition of 'aggravated felony'
Establishes a post-proceeding removal relief process for affected aliens.
Document 5: Authorizes the cancellation of removal for certain minors who meet specific criteria
Sets conditions for maintaining conditional resident status.
Document 6: Permits P-1 visa nonimmigrant athletes to renew their admission in five-year increments.
Document 7: Establishes a visa waiver program for the U.S. Virgin Islands for certain nationals.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the assessment and accountability of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation, protection, and management of wilderness areas in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, natural, wilderness, historic, scenic, riparian, geological, educational, and scientific values found within the National Monument.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
Document 2: Amends the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 to designate certain lands in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness.'

Directs the Secretary to seek to enter into a memorandum of understanding with the Coquille Indian tribe regarding access to the Copper Salmon Wilderness to conduct historical and cultural activities.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey, to the local communities in South Dakota that are currently managing specified community cemeteries, all interest of the United States in: (1) the parcels of National Forest System land that contain the cemeteries.
Allows for the expansion of those cemeteries.
Document 4: This bill amends the Wilderness Act to prohibit the Department of Agriculture from adopting, amending, or enforcing any regulations that would delay emergency response personnel from entering into a wilderness area.
Any entrance by federal, state, or local emergency response personnel into a wilderness area in response to an emergency shall be reported to the appropriate Department within 24 hours.
Document 5: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Sets forth provisions governing continued operations at the Grand River Ditch.
Document 6: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Requires the development of separate management plans for such areas.
Document 7: This bill amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness.'

topic.name: Child and Elder Abuse Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing abuse, neglect, and exploitation of vulnerable populations, particularly children and the elderly.

Document 1: Elder Protection and Abuse Prevention Act
address elder justice and exploitation, and prevent abuse, neglect, and exploitation of older adults.
Document 2: Rehab and Ahmed Amer Foster Care Improvement Act

Document 3: Internet Abuse, Stalking, and Domestic Violence Prevention Act

Document 4: Missing Children's Assistance Act
operate a cyber tipline to report Internet-related child sexual exploitation.
Document 5: Domestic Violence Judicial Support Act
authorize grants for supervised visitation in situations involving domestic violence and child abuse.
Document 6: Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act
improve the assessment and investigation of suspected child abuse cases.
Document 7: Riya Wilson Act
require state child welfare agency to report information on missing or abducted children.

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on improving mental health services, support for individuals with mental illnesses, and protection for vulnerable populations such as the elderly and those with autism.

Document 1: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for demonstration projects to provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses
to provide integrated treatment services for individuals with a serious mental illness and a co-occurring substance abuse disorder.

increases public awareness of elder abuse and financial exploitation.
Document 3: authorize the use of specified funds to provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions for individuals with mental illness
include in a state plan for comprehensive community-based mental health systems support services for individuals released from inpatient or residential institutions.
Document 4: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants or cooperative agreements to states to develop plans for the statewide coordination of services to assist adolescents and young adults with a serious mental health disorder
provide technical assistance to the states that are planning or implementing programs under this Act.
Document 5: revise provisions related to the Interagency Autism Coordinating Committee
require the Secretary of HHS to establish grant programs related to autism spectrum disorders.
Document 6: authorize treatment of autism spectrum disorders, if a health care professional determines that such treatment is medically necessary.
Document 7: require the Director of the National Institutes of Health to take certain actions regarding eating disorder research
provide for education and training on eating disorders.

topic.name: Prescription Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the regulation, safety, and efficacy of prescription drugs and addressing issues related to opioid abuse and drug interactions.

Document 1: establish national interoperability standards to facilitate the exchange of prescription information
ensure that such standards support transmissions that are fully secured
Document 2: Deems the approved application for the drug mifepristone to have been withdrawn
directs the Comptroller General to review and report on the process by which the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved mifepristone
Document 3: allows the holder of an approved abbreviated new drug application to change the labeling of a drug
conforming changes to the labeling of the equivalent listed drug
Document 4: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to designate a combination of drugs as a significant drug combination
requires the Secretary to review and take action on a drug in a significant drug combination
Document 5: requires HHS to convene a Pain Management Best Practices Inter-Agency Task Force
advance education and awareness regarding the risk of abuse of prescription opioids
Document 6: establish training guidelines for federal health care facilities
prescribing opioid overdose reversal drugs
Document 7: require HHS to encourage health care facilities to report on antibacterial drug use
permit the FDA to approve an antibiotic drug for use in a limited population

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Restoration
topic.description: Legislative acts focused on the planning, design, construction, and restoration of water resources and ecosystems in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the city of San Juan Capistrano, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system.
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of a project to expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities.
Document 2: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement between the Hualapai Tribe, the Department of the Interior, the Arizona Game and Fish Commission, the Arizona Department of Water Resources, and the Freeport Minerals Corporation.
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality, Indian River Lagoon, South Florida.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, Picayune Strand, Collier County, Florida.
Document 4: Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control, water supply storage, outdoor recreation enhancements.
Authorizes the Chief to construct demonstration projects to provide information to develop the study.
Document 6: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with Pima County, Arizona, to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.

topic.name: Mortgage Refinancing and Foreclosure Prevention
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at facilitating mortgage refinancing and preventing foreclosure for homeowners in financial distress.

Document 1: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Specifies the terms and conditions of a refinancing mortgage, including a 40-year term to maturity and a prohibition on borrower fees.
Document 2: Authorizes a bankruptcy court to establish a loss mitigation program for the negotiation of consensual alternatives to avoid foreclosure between an individual debtor and the holder of a claim secured by a security interest in the debtor's principal residence.
Document 3: Prohibits commencement of a foreclosure action with respect to a federally related residential mortgage loan secured by a first or subordinate lien unless the person commencing the foreclosure complies with specified requirements pertaining to borrower notification and assistance from a HUD-approved housing counseling agency.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and acts aimed at the conservation and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 3: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 4: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out, either individually or cooperatively, prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 5: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects, including monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds, in addition to projects addressing emerging priorities or threats.
Authorizes the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing support and assistance to the agricultural sector, particularly in the context of disaster relief, resource management, and rural development.

Document 1: Eliminate Auto Test (EAT) Act of 2011 - Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to exclude the value of vehicles used for household transportation, or to obtain or continue employment, from the resource limitation for purposes of supplemental nutrition assistance (SNAP, formerly food stamps) eligibility.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide cost-share payments to agricultural producers for water conservation projects on farms involving the construction, improvement, or maintenance of irrigation ponds and the implementation of other measures that improve groundwater and surface water conservation.
Document 3: Farmers Disaster Assistance Act of 2011 - Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to make supplemental payments to agricultural producers receiving direct payments for covered commodities, direct payments for peanuts, or average crop revenue election (ACRE) payments in disaster counties that had qualifying losses for the 2011 crop year.
Document 4: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act to state that the delivery by a producer or association of producers of raw milk to a handler under a federal milk marketing order shall be deemed to occur when the raw milk is received at the producer's farm.
Document 5: Rural Education Achievement Program Reauthorization Act of 2007 - Amends part B (Rural Education Initiative) of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program.
Document 6: Wildfire and Drought Relief for Ranchers and Farmers Act of 2012 - Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to use Commodity Credit Corporation funds in FY2012 for the following supplemental agricultural disaster programs.
Document 7: Farm-to-Fuel Investment Act of 2007 - Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to designate as bioenergy cropsheds areas that are physically located within a 50-mile radius, or some other economically-practicable distance, from an existing or planned biorefinery.

Document 6: conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes
potential to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles
Document 7: authorize appropriations to the Secretary of Energy for research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of vehicles and related technologies
ensure that the Department of Energy continues to support research, development, engineering, demonstration, and commercial application activities

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the protection and security of personally identifiable information and ensuring public access to government reports.

Document 1: requires the Government Publishing Office (GPO) to establish and maintain a website that provides the public free electronic access to all congressionally mandated reports
Each federal agency shall publish congressionally mandated reports on such website.
Document 2: Defines 'identity theft' to mean a fraud committed using the sensitive personal information of another individual
Requires any agency or person that owns or licenses computerized data containing sensitive personal information to develop, implement, and maintain reasonable security and notification procedures.
Document 3: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form
Amends the federal criminal code to make fraud in connection with the unauthorized access of personally identifiable information a predicate for instituting a prosecution for racketeering.
Document 4: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security
known to have resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 5: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information.
Document 6: permits a video tape service provider to obtain a consumer's consent, which is required to disclose personally identifiable information concerning the consumer
in advance for a set period or until such consent is withdrawn.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 to reestablish the oversight authority of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget

Requires the Secretary to develop and oversee implementation of operational directives requiring agencies to implement the Director's standards and guidelines for safeguarding federal information and systems.

Document 6: Effective Corruption Prosecutions Act of 2007

Document 7: Safeguarding Social Security Numbers Act of 2008
